description:
You are a Solutions Architect at a cloud consulting firm. A client is launching a multi-tenant SaaS application on AWS and needs a formal architecture diagram for their security review board. Using the requirements in ~/Desktop/saas_arch_requirements.txt, create a complete AWS architecture diagram in draw.io using the AWS shape library with the following components:

NETWORK LAYER: 1 VPC spanning 2 Availability Zones, each AZ with 1 public subnet and 1 private subnet (4 subnets total), an Internet Gateway, and 1 NAT Gateway in each public subnet.
COMPUTE LAYER: Application Load Balancer in the public subnets, 2 EC2 instances (one per AZ) in private subnets behind the ALB, and an Auto Scaling Group.
DATA LAYER: RDS Multi-AZ instance (PostgreSQL) and ElastiCache Redis cluster in private subnets, and an S3 bucket for static assets.
EDGE/CDN LAYER: CloudFront distribution in front of the ALB and a Route 53 hosted zone.
SECURITY: Security Group boundaries represented as dashed rectangles around (a) public subnets, (b) private subnets, and (c) the data layer.

Include a second page titled 'Data Flow' showing the request path: User -> CloudFront -> ALB -> EC2 -> RDS/ElastiCache as a sequence of labeled arrows. Save the file as ~/Desktop/aws_architecture.drawio and export page 1 as ~/Desktop/aws_architecture.png.

Login: username=ga, password=password123
